rag_rules: When AGREE diagnostics report unresolved references or type errors, trace the issue to the first invalid identifier or helper declaration on the same line and fix the root cause.
Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers within the same annex to avoid 'named thing must be an expression with a type' errors.
Ensure AGREE scalar types match AADL expectations: use int, real, and bool for constants and equations.
generic_rules: In AGREE contracts, always resolve references to visible features or declared helpers before using them in expressions.
Validate that all AGREE type declarations align with the expected scalar types (int, real, bool) to prevent type mismatch errors.
When fixing AGREE diagnostics, prioritize correcting root causes rather than adding new architecture elements.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be placed in valid annex scopes: component types for assumptions/guarantees, implementations for bindings.
Assignments in implementation scopes must reference existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or types that are not present in the AADL model or its dependencies.
Avoid placing guarantee statements in implementation scopes; use type scopes for output dependency contracts.
